evidence_excerpt: At last review at 8 months, plasma sodium was normal (137 mmol/L) and weight gain was established, but weight remained <3rd centile and salt supplements were still required; eczema persisted.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: At 8 months, plasma sodium was normal on continued sodium chloride supplements; diffuse dry eczematous skin persisted though somewhat improved.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Within a week of treatment, plasma sodium normalised and sodium was detected in the urine.
She was discharged home at 3 months of age on sodium chloride supplements with an established weight gain.
At last review at 8 months of age, plasma sodium was normal (137 mmol/L).

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact date of definitive diagnosis is not explicitly documented; the start of sodium chloride supplements at 10 weeks was used as the proxy index (diagnosis/treatment plan). Follow-up is limited to 8 months, so long-term beyond 12 months is not available. Functional status in an infant is inferred conservatively from growth and clinical management rather than explicit developmental assessments.
